children.value: It appears that every iterate is just returning the next index, so it is just a for loop.
I first translated it into python with chatgpt.

props.code:
def try_flag(flag):
    flag = [ord(i) for i in flag]
    counter1 = 0
    counter2 = 0

    final_str = []

    def iterate(i):
        nonlocal counter1, counter2, final_str

        char = flag[i]
        v4 = (i & 1) != 0
        v1 = 0x60 < char <= 0x7A

        if v1:
            rotated = (char >> table2[counter2]) | (char << (8 - table2[counter2]))
            flag[i] = rotated & 0xFF  # Ensure byte size
        else:
            rotated = ((char << 6) | (char >> 2)) ^ table1[counter1]
            flag[i] = rotated & 0xFF  # Ensure byte size

        if (i & 1) == 0:
            flag[i] = flag[i] * int(v1)
        else:
            if v1:
                flag[i] = (char ^ table1[counter1]) & 0xFF  # Ensure byte size
            else:
                flag[i] = ((4 * char) | (char >> 6)) & 0xFF  # Ensure byte size

        counter1 = (v4 + counter1) % 6
        counter2 = (v4 + counter2) % 6
        # print(f"{flag[i]:02x},", end='')
        return i + 1

    i = 0
    while i < len(flag):
        i = iterate(i)
    print(' '.join([f"{i:02x}" for i in flag]))
    return flag
props.className: language-python shiki shiki-themes one-light one-dark-pro monokai

children.value: And found out that all characters are modified independently of each other, that's great.
